candidateAtoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.OrderOtherController
candidateAtoms.object: @CrossOrigin origins=* applied per-endpoint on orderOther routes

candidateAtoms.subject: ts-order-service.OrderController
candidateAtoms.object: @CrossOrigin origins=* applied per-endpoint on order routes

candidateContexts.label: ts-notification-service mail dispatch flow (service to MailService to JavaMailSender)
ts-order-other-service order lifecycle (controller, security, service, repository, entity)
ts-order-service order endpoints (controller and security surface observed in this chunk; service/repository not in chunk)

candidateCovers.label: order vs orderOther shared convention measurement surface (mirrored route shape, response envelope, CORS/JWT security pattern, order-status-code and money-amount vocabulary)

notes: small single-method rabbit publish wrapper, no controller wiring evidence in this chunk
CrudRepository interface over NotifyInfo, no custom finder methods
freemarker template rendering into MimeMessage then JavaMailSender.send
four near-identical try/catch-around-sendEmail methods, boolean return means dispatch attempted without an exception
mail password has an embedded literal default overridable via env var
broad permitAll matcher on the orderOther wildcard is declared before the role-restricted POST/PUT/DELETE matchers on the same path family
16 REST endpoints under /api/v1/orderOtherService, thin pass-through to service layer
equals() second conjunct compares boughtDate against the other order's travelDate rather than its boughtDate
largest file in the chunk: 20 public methods, one cross-service RestTemplate call to ts-station-service, and the money/order-status-code conventions used for the cech section atoms come from here
rabbitmq/stream binding block is fully commented out in this file
matcher declaration order here is the reverse of ts-order-other-service.SecurityConfig: role-restricted matchers precede the permitAll wildcard matcher
route shape is structurally identical to OrderOtherController with orderservice/order in place of orderOtherService/orderOther; OrderService/OrderServiceImpl themselves are not in this chunk

privateUnavailableNotes: ts-order-service OrderService interface and OrderServiceImpl are referenced by the controller and security config in this chunk but were not part of the assigned worklist rows, so the order-service side of the shared order/orderOther convention comparison is only partially observed here.
